title: NZ School Holidays Travel Tips — How to Beat the Crowds and Save Money
meta_desc: Discover NZ school holidays travel tips to save money and avoid crowds with smart booking strategies
intro: If you're planning to travel during the upcoming school holidays, be prepared for peak pricing and crowds at popular destinations like Rotorua and Queenstown. However, with some smart planning, you can beat the crowds and save up to $500 on a family holiday. In this article, we'll share our top tips for navigating NZ school holidays travel.

sections.heading: Plan Ahead to Save Money
sections.body: Booking your accommodation and activities well in advance can make a significant difference in prices. For example, a family room at the Novotel Rotorua Lakeside can cost up to $450 per night during peak season, but booking 6 months ahead can reduce the price to $320 per night.

sections.heading: Top Destinations for School Holidays
list: Bay of Islands (3-hour drive from Auckland)
Abel Tasman National Park (4-hour drive from Nelson)

sections.heading: Save Money on Activities
sections.body: Instead of booking expensive guided tours, consider self-guided activities like hiking or visiting local museums. For example, the Rotorua Museum costs $20 per adult and $5 per child, while a guided tour of Wai-O-Tapu can cost up to $80 per person.

sections.heading: Get the Best Deals with Our Tools
